title: Rape-murder of 3-year-old: Kin seek death, protest shuts down Pune district
description: A brutal rape and murder of a three-year-old girl has sparked outrage in Pune's Bhor-Rajgad belt, leading to a shutdown. The victim's family refuses to meet politicians until the 65-year-old accused, a repeat offender, is sentenced to death. Protests erupted, with the Chief Minister assuring a fast-track trial and pursuit of the death penalty, while cautioning against public execution demands.

title: Vijay Election Results 2026 Live Updates: DMK leads as TVK pushes AIADMK to third in several seats, counting underway
Shivang Kumar: How father transformed the swashbuckling opener into a chinaman in a year!
description: Shivang Kumar made his name in the Madhya Pradesh T20 League as an opener. His exploits with the bat helped him become a marquee player for Bundelkhand Bulls, who picked him for Rs 13 lakh for the 2026 season. But within a year, he learnt the art of wrist-spin from his father, a former Bengal U-19 cricketer, and made instant impact. The IPL franchises noticed and multiple trials later, he was roped in by the Sunrisers Hyderabad.

title: Middle East crisis: Iran proposes 30-day plan to end war, says it has received US reply
description: Iran has received a US response to its 30-day peace proposal, conveyed via Pakistan. The US President expressed doubt about the proposal's success, citing Iran's past actions.

title: As many say software is dead, Amazon Web Services CEO Matt Garman calls it a 'huge ...'
description: AWS CEO Matt Garman views the AI-driven 'SaaSpocalypse' as a major business opportunity, not a threat.  He highlighted the rise of agentic AI reshaping work and announced new AI-powered tools like the Quick desktop app, aiming to revolutionize personal productivity.  AWS is also deepening its OpenAI partnership, integrating advanced AI models into its cloud services to meet surging demand.

More Strait talks: Trump rolls out 'Project Freedom' to guide ships through Hormuz
description: US President Donald Trump launched 'Project Freedom' to aid commercial ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, citing humanitarian concerns and low supplies.  He stated the US will guide vessels safely out, emphasizing these ships belong to nations uninvolved in regional disputes. Trump also revealed positive discussions with Iran, though he rejected their latest proposal for conflict resolution.

title: Are Huma Qureshi and Rachit Singh set to get married in October this year? - Report
description: Huma Qureshi and Rachit Singh are reportedly planning to tie the knot in October or November of 2026. Following a private engagement in the US, the couple is said to be organizing an intimate wedding ceremony, likely in Mumbai, with a grand reception for industry friends to follow. Their relationship gained attention after they were seen together at various events. Read on to know more in detail.

title: India-Nepal border: Crossing a line
description: With Nepalese law enforcement agencies pushing for customs duty on Indian goods above ₹100, people living along the Indo-Nepal border are alarmed over a disruption to their lives. title: Air India CEO race narrows to 4 names
description: The race for Air India's CEO position has narrowed to four candidates, including two Indian executives, Nipun Aggarwal and Vinod Kannan, and two expatriates. The airline's board is set to meet soon to make a decision amidst significant financial losses and operational challenges. The delay in appointing a successor to Campbell Wilson is reportedly adding to the airline's ongoing struggles.

title: PSL final: Mohsin Naqvi hands trophy as Babar Azam lifts first T20 title as captain

description: A 14-year-old Sudanese boy received a life-saving heart transplant in Delhi, thanks to a donor heart flown from Haryana. The organ came from a 41-year-old woman declared brain dead, whose family bravely consented to donation.

title: Abandoned in forest, 2-year-old walks for 20 hours, survives
description: A two-year-old boy miraculously survived nearly 20 hours alone in a wild animal-infested forest after his father allegedly murdered his mother and abandoned him. Found weak and dehydrated by police, the toddler's incredible resilience in the face of extreme danger has stunned authorities. The father has been arrested, and the child is recovering.

title: India dismisses Nepal’s Kailash yatra objection over Lipulekh Pass
description: Nepal has officially objected to India resuming the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra through Lipulekh Pass, a territory it claims based on the 1816 Sugauli Treaty. India dismissed these claims as unjustified and historically unfounded, stating Lipulekh has been a long-standing yatra route. This dispute marks a potential test for bilateral ties as both nations prepare for diplomatic discussions.

title: SG Tushar Mehta takes swipe at ‘bully’ judges in his book
description: Senior advocate Tushar Mehta's upcoming book criticizes some judges for exhibiting 'judicial bullying' due to excessive courtroom reverence. He argues that while judges face immense pressure, this cannot justify incivility, as the public expects high standards from an institution they cannot easily remove. Mehta highlights the power imbalance lawyers face when confronting arrogant judges.

title: Antonelli takes third win a row for Mercedes to stretch F1 lead in Miami
description: The youngest driver ever to lead the championship, ⁠at 19 years old, Kimi Antonelli also became the first to take his first three career wins from the top slot on the grid and also the first in five editions of the race to win ‌in Miami from the front row

title: Bengal SIR a ‘threat’ to our jobs: Professionals, government staff move court
description: Livelihoods are at stake as 27 lakh voters in Bengal are stripped of voting rights due to electoral roll deletions during SIR. Professionals, including a PSU officer, teacher, doctor, and insurance agent, have moved the Calcutta High Court, fearing their citizenship will be questioned, potentially leading to job termination and licence cancellation. Their appeals for urgent hearings were denied.

title: May the Fourth be with you
description: Do not force push these pages away because you can’t. Decades may pass but one thing is rock — we are never ever letting go of lightsabers, starfighters, or stormtroopers.

title: I am one of Brij Bhushan's six victims, reveals wrestler Vinesh Phogat
description: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat revealed she is one of six accusers of former WFI chief Brij Bhushan Singh. Her return to competition in his stronghold, Gonda, raises concerns about fairness and potential bias in officiating. Phogat, now a legislator, demands transparency, holding the government accountable for any incidents. The WFI president assures safety and denies bias.

title: Independent journalism punished, compliance rewarded under current regime, says Kharge
description: Sections of the media have been reduced to echoing the ruling establishment, while those who persist in asking questions are ‘targeted relentlessly’, says the Congress president while calling for deep introspection by all stakeholders on the occasion of World Press Freedom Day

title: US goes ahead with radar upgrade for Pakistan’s F-16 fleet, move a worry for India
description: The US is proceeding with a $488 million radar upgrade for Pakistan's F-16 fighter jets, a move expected to heighten Indian security concerns. This program, contracted to Northrop Grumman, will enhance Pakistan's air capabilities amidst regional tensions. India's own fighter squadron numbers are reportedly below the required strength for a two-front conflict, making this development particularly noteworthy.

title: Admiral Tripathi meets Myanmar naval leadership, discusses steps to boost cooperation
description: Indian Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi concluded a visit to Myanmar, reinforcing defence ties and navy-to-navy cooperation. Discussions focused on enhancing training exchanges and operational engagements, including the handover of Indian-assisted maritime security equipment. This collaboration underscores India's strategic importance of Myanmar under its 'Neighbourhood First' policy.

title: Prohibitory orders imposed in Doda as AAP MLA returns home district after 8 months in jail
description: The lone AAP legislator in the Union Territory was arrested on September 8, 2025, following his ‘inappropriate remarks’ against then-Deputy Commissioner Harvinder Singh over the shifting of a health centre at Doda’s Kencha, and was booked under the Public Safety Act that allows preventive detention up to two years

title: Sai's 57, pacers help Gujarat Titans pull off tense win over Punjab Kings
description: Gujarat Titans edged past Punjab Kings by four wickets in a thrilling IPL clash. Sai Sudharsan anchored the chase with 57, while Washington Sundar stayed unbeaten on 40 to seal victory in the final over. Earlier, debutant Suryansh Shedge scored a fighting 57 for Punjab after early damage caused by Mohammed Siraj, Jason Holder and Kagiso Rabada.

Assembly elections 2026: From East to South, key battlegrounds to watch out for
description: After the counting begins and trends trickle in, these seats offer early clues to momentum, alliance cohesion and voter sentiment that can ripple across regions. From prestige contests involving chief ministers to urban battlegrounds testing new entrants, the outcomes in these constituencies will help explain whether incumbency holds or fractures. They also reflect how local issues such as welfare delivery, identity, urban infrastructure and leadership credibility interact with wider political narratives.

title: 'I don't know': Kapil Dev declines to comment on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
description: Former Indian all-rounder Kapil Dev declined to comment on teenage IPL star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, saying experts like Sunil Gavaskar and Ravi Shastri are better suited to judge him. The 15-year-old Rajasthan Royals opener has scored 404 runs in IPL 2026, including a 36-ball century, earning widespread praise and India call-up discussions.

title: Assembly poll results 2026: A defining test for BJP, Cong & regional satraps
description: As vote counting looms for five key states, the 2026 assembly elections represent a critical juncture for national parties and regional strongholds. The BJP seeks geographic expansion, while the Congress fights for relevance. Regional forces face mounting pressure, making the outcome a test of India's political trajectory.

title: As Accenture rolls out Microsoft Copilot to staff, CEO says teams doing higher-value work
description: Microsoft's Copilot 365 AI assistant is now powering Accenture's workforce of 743,000 employees, marking the largest enterprise deal for the chatbot. This significant adoption by Accenture, a leading AI adopter, highlights Microsoft's strategy to boost paid Copilot subscriptions. Early results show employees completing routine tasks up to 15 times faster, with major productivity gains reported.

‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
description: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: From red to green to saffron? BJP eyes power shift in Bengal as Mamata defends her turf
description: West Bengal's political landscape, historically dominated by single parties for extended periods, is now witnessing an intense battle for the 2026 assembly elections. Chief minister Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule faces a formidable challenge from the aggressively expanding BJP, which aims to end the TMC's reign. This election marks a critical juncture for the state's political legacy.
